untranslatable_reason: Signal requires direct comparison of IV vs realized volatility, theoretical option pricing models, delta rebalancing triggers, and P&L threshold monitoring. These are derivatives/options-specific metrics not available in the standard indicator set (hvol_20 approximates realized vol, india_vix approximates IV, but no direct IV-RV comparison, theoretical value calculations, or options decay modeling available). Entry thresholds for 'IV discount', 'margin of safety %', and 'delta drift' cannot be operationalized with price-based indicators alone.
translation_notes: Attempted proxy: hvol_20 (realized volatility proxy) compared to india_vix (IV proxy) for vol regime detection, combined with price proximity to SMA_20 as liquidity/volatility context. Exit on IV-RV convergence approximated as hvol approaching india_vix. However, this loses critical Natenberg concepts: (1) time decay race vs adjustment gains, (2) purchase price relative to theoretical value, (3) delta rebalancing frequency as trigger. Full implementation requires options Greeks (delta, theta, vega) and mark-to-model pricing not in allowed indicator set.
